meta.description: New brand color system replacing the existing warm-orange/cream palette with a bolder spicy-paprika/saffron/marine scheme

previousPalette.note: Original design used a soft terracotta-orange primary, warm cream backgrounds, dark olive text, and white cards. The new palette intensifies the orange toward a fiery paprika, introduces a saffron secondary, adds a marine-blue accent, and anchors darks with a warm charcoal-brown.

charcoal-brown.role: Primary dark — body text, dark section backgrounds, footer

spicy-paprika.role: Brand primary — CTAs, logo accent, hero headline accent, icons, active states

deep-saffron.role: Brand secondary — hero headline highlight word, hover states, inline highlights, progress connectors

bright-marine.role: Accent / tertiary — formula category labels, hyperlinks, secondary CTAs, informational badges

black.role: Maximum contrast — display headings on light backgrounds, hero primary headline

derivedTokens.note: Derived tokens extend the core 5-color palette with functional variants — tints, shades, and alphas — needed to build the full component system.

--bg-base.derivedFrom: charcoal-brown at 3% opacity on white — warm off-white, replacing old cream
--bg-base.usage: Page base background, section alternating background

--bg-surface.usage: Cards, modals, navbar, pricing card, FAQ items

--bg-dark-elevated.usage: Cards inside dark sections, hover state on dark nav items

--bg-brand-subtle.usage: Feature icon container background, CTA section tint, hover on ghost buttons

--bg-saffron-subtle.usage: Highlight chip backgrounds, badge backgrounds in light sections

--bg-marine-subtle.usage: Formula category label background, info alert background

--text-primary.usage: Body copy, card descriptions, FAQ answers, nav links

--text-heading.usage: H1, H2, H3 — section titles, hero headline primary line

--text-muted.usage: Subtext, captions, stats labels, pricing feature fine print

--text-brand.usage: Hero accent word ('Before the Exam'), section eyebrow labels, checklist ticks

--text-saffron.usage: Hero secondary accent ('Make It Count'), dark-section checklist bullets, sub-taglines

--text-marine.usage: Inline links, formula card reading label, secondary action text

--text-inverse.usage: All text on dark/brand backgrounds — CTA banner, footer, dark feature section, filled buttons

--text-inverse-muted.usage: Footer subtext, copyright, secondary text on dark backgrounds

--border-default.derivedFrom: warm neutral — charcoal-brown at 12% on white
--border-default.usage: Card borders, dividers, FAQ item separators, input outlines

--border-brand.usage: Pricing card featured border, active tab underline, focus rings

--border-dark.usage: Dividers inside dark sections and footer

--focus-ring.usage: Focus outline on all interactive elements (keyboard nav)

headlineLine2.note: Spicy-paprika replaces the old terracotta orange — more vivid, higher energy

headlineLine3.note: Could alternatively use #ff9505 (deep-saffron) for a warm-to-hot gradient feel across line 2→3

accentWord.note: The italic/colored accent in 'Everything you need to ace Level 1'

headlineAccent.note: Deep-saffron replaces the old orange on dark — better contrast ratio on #353531 (4.8:1) vs white

readingLabel.note: Bright-marine label — the only place in the design where marine blue appears on light; creates informational hierarchy

cssCustomProperties.note: Drop these into :root {} — they cover every token needed to theme the full site

contrastChecks.wcag: AA for large text / UI components (≥3:1). Use for icons, borders, large headings. Avoid for small body text.
contrastChecks.recommendation: Use --text-brand (#ec4e20) only at font-size ≥18px or font-weight ≥700 on white backgrounds.

contrastChecks.wcag: Fails AA for text. Safe only for decorative elements, thick borders, and icon fills.
contrastChecks.recommendation: Do NOT use --color-saffron as text color on white/light backgrounds. Use it on dark (#353531) backgrounds where it achieves 6.4:1.

contrastChecks.wcag: AAA — excellent. Primary use case for saffron text.
contrastChecks.recommendation: Ideal for headlines and checklist items in dark sections.
Safe for inline links, formula labels, and secondary CTAs.

contrastChecks.wcag: AAA — excellent for all body text.
AA for large text / buttons (≥3:1). Acceptable for CTA buttons with ≥14px bold.
contrastChecks.recommendation: Use white text on paprika buttons only with bold weight (≥600) and min 14px.

keyGuidelines: Never use deep-saffron as body text on light backgrounds — decorative/icon use only
Spicy-paprika on light is safe for large headlines (≥24px) and UI elements (icons, borders)
Bright-marine is the safest accent for text/links on light — use it freely for informational labels
All interactive elements need visible focus rings using --focus-ring

to.primary: Spicy paprika (#ec4e20) — more saturated, slightly redder, higher energy
to.background: Warm off-white (#faf8f4) — cleaner but still warm
to.text: Charcoal brown (#353531) — slightly warmer than pure charcoal
to.darkSection: Charcoal brown (#353531) — consistent with text dark
to.accent: Deep saffron (#ff9505) for warm secondary + Bright marine (#016fb9) for informational blue

changeLog.impactSummary: The rebrand intensifies the brand energy (paprika vs terracotta), adds a saffron warm-secondary for layered warmth in dark sections, and introduces a marine-blue functional accent that adds informational depth to formula cards and links — a previously absent color dimension.
